Butt Bump Ending.title: Butt Bump Ending
Butt Bump Ending.message: You miscalculate. That is the explanation for what happens next, because suddenly Dr. Bennet is thrown to the floor, as a result of your devastating, explosive hip check. You only catch a glimpse of her completely shocked and baffled expression before Darius tackles you and drags your drunk ass out of Pioneer Hall. Whoops. Maybe if you'd stopped after the first one this wouldn't have happened. This is probably a habit worth breaking.

Quit Before You Even Started Ending.title: Quit Before You Even Started Ending
Quit Before You Even Started Ending.message: Dang. You didn't even last long enough to say hi. Hey, you know what, everyone has long days. You'll get em next time, but until then you'll be unemployed. Just food for thought.

2048 Ending.title: 2048 Ending
2048 Ending.message: Maybe you were nervous, but you got distracted and spent the rest of the evening on the comfortable couch, sipping wine and playing 2048 on your phone. It was a lovely evening, but you still don't have an internship.

Oh, Gross Ending.title: Oh, Gross Ending
Oh, Gross Ending.message:
You don't know if it's the huge amounts of wine you've been drinking, or the fact that the cheese cubes you've been snacking on have been left unrefrigerated for too long, or maybe you're simply that nervous. Whatever the reason, you unexpectedly vomit with such force that it creates a beautiful parabola across the space between you and Dr. Bennet. You've been facing her this whole time, and neither of you are fast enough to stop the contents of your stomach from liberally decorating the front of her crisp blue pantsuit.

Somebody grabs your shoulder from behind. It's Darius, and he goes, 'Ella! I only gave you one glass of wine!' and you try to shoot back a witty retort, but instead of words, liquid comes out. The front of his white shirt becomes not white.

Someone calls you an Uber and sends you home clutching a wastebasket to your face. Apparently, Darius and Dr. Bennet end up having a nice bonding moment when they both go to the lounge sink to wash your barf out of their formalwear. You learn a few days later that during this sink-washing, Darius charmed Dr. Bennet enough to get the number of a high-ranking UN official. The news makes you want to barf for a different reason.

Invasion of Privacy Ending.title: Invasion of Privacy Ending
Invasion of Privacy Ending.message:
You're hoping to learn more about the elusive Dr. Elizabeth Bennet; if you know more about her, surely you'll be able to more carefully curate the topics of conversation. Without her knowing what you're doing, you start scrolling through many pictures. Most of them are useless, uninteresting photos of her children and family, but one of them from a few months prior catches your eye.

It's a photo of Dr. Bennet wearing a big EVA spacesuit, looking serious. You're aware of the existing models of spacesuits, but none of them look as sleek and advanced as the one in this picture. It's outfitted with lots of new gear that you don't recognize, and it looks cool as hell.

In your endless stupidity, you show her and ask her what the suit is called, and she immediately goes, 'Oh, my God. You can't be seeing this, you're not allowed - why would you ever think it was okay to go through my camera roll? What is your problem?'

Turns out that by viewing the picture, you've not only compromised Dr. Bennet's privacy, but you've also compromised some very sensitive, very highly protected information from the NASA astronaut training process. Dr. Bennet glares and then walks away from you, rapidly talking to someone on the phone, and doesn't speak to ANYONE for the rest of the evening. You get a lot of ugly looks, and nobody accompanies you on your walk home later.

As you walk alone in the dark, you get stopped by two agents in crisp suits, with wires in their ears. They invite you into a big black car with tinted windows. Inside the car, they ask you lots of questions. You think for a moment that they might blindfold you and bring you to the embassy, but after only a few questions they let you go, and disappear into the night. You go home normally, and the event seems to resolve itself. Nobody brings it up again.

But every once in a while, when you are walking around in the city, or studying late at the library, or sitting in a cafe, you think you see a man in a suit watching you through a window, or from across the street. When you go to look at them again, they're gone, making you question if they were ever really there.

You'd criticize them for invading your privacy, but you really don't have the moral high ground on that one.

Tackle Ending.title: The Tackle Ending
Tackle Ending.message:
A cheerful voice on the other end says 'Hi, this is Brock Bennet, who is this?' but before you can respond, Elizabeth Bennet deftly grabs the phone back, and at the same time your good friend David Razo tackles you from behind and prevents you from doing any more weird BS. David is a student athlete, and so despite your thrashing, he easily picks you up over his head, spins you like a pizza, and throws you over the bike shed into the swamp.

Dr. Bennet high-fives him, and then complements him on the accuracy of his launch. 'That was a textbook tackle, David. I'm really impressed. You know, I have a lot of respect for athletes. I think that the discipline and work ethic it takes to be a student athlete is really admirable. And what a well-engineered throw! The timing, the trajectory! You seem like a really great guy, David. Do you have an internship lined up for the summer?'

Injury 5 Ending.title: Injury 5 Ending
Injury 5 Ending.message: Dr. Bennet's knee-jerk reaction to your sudden attack is to perform a well-placed heel strike to your face. The force of the kick knocks out several of your teeth, which the dentist later informs you cannot be reattached. You spend the rest of your time at ISU attempting to network with a diabolical lisp.

First Contact Ending.title: First Contact
First Contact Ending.message: SUCCESS! You did great, and she actually gave you a next task - you now have an email to reach out to. It turns out that the recipient is excited to hear from you, and you schedule a Zoom call to hear about their career and to see if they can help you with yours. Good work champ!

100 Percent Ending.title: The Perfect Run
100 Percent Ending.message:
SUCCESS! You knocked it out of the park, and not only acquired a contact from her but also gave her your LinkedIn. Well done! She smiles and heads off to the next person, but you actually exchange smiles with her twice more before the night is out. The conversation flowed so naturally that you almost forgot you were talking to someone who has been to space. You feel AMAZING.

On your way back to the snack table, a man intercepts you: a faculty member at ESA who overheard your conversation with Dr. Bennet and was, apparently, intrigued. 'I work on that exact project,' he says, handing you his card. 'We have a summer internship that keeps losing good candidates to SpaceX. I think you might be exactly the kind of person we're looking for.' He writes his PERSONAL email on the back of the card. 'Please do send me your CV.'

What's more, on the way out to your car, you run into Dr. Bennet and her husband in the parking lot, where they're standing and scratching their heads next to their rental car with the hood up. Dr. Bennet catches your eye and quickly approaches YOU this time, seemingly apologetic. '____, sorry to bother you, I'm sure you're exhausted and trying to get home too, but I'm wondering if I could ask you a favor.'

You try to pretend like this is even slightly the case and that you are not overjoyed for another opportunity to speak with her. 'Oh, yeah, no worries in the least. What's going on?'

'Our car battery seem to have died,' says her husband from behind her, 'and if you had jumper cables, and could try jumping it for us, we'd be so, so appreciative.'

You DO have jumper cables, and your own car's battery dies so often that you are familiar with the process. Brock Bennet stands behind you with his arms crossed as you, hands shaking, execute the battery revival perfectly on the first try. Dr. Bennet tries her key in the ignition, and it revs up easily.

Dr. Bennet's husband heartily shakes your hand. 'Thank you so, so much, ____. We really appreciate it. You're definitely my favorite ISU student tonight.'

Dr. Bennet gets out of the car and goes, 'Brock, you can't say that. We're not going to play favorites. But,' she adds, 'you seem very talented at electrical engineering, in addition to the other stuff we talked about. If the opportunity comes up, I'll do my best to recommend you to the right people.'

You thank them copiously, and exchange more pleasantries, and then drive home feeling like a million bucks. Which is just as well, because on the walk from your car to your apartment, you spot a twenty-euro note on the pavement. You look around. There is absolutely no one. It's just yours now.

When you get back to the residence, your roommate is sitting on the floor with a box holding approximately 20 pieces of cheesecake. 'I misread the order form,' they say, gesturing helplessly at the cake. 'Please. Take as many as you want. I am begging you.' You take two. They taste incredible. They are still cold.

You check your email before bed and discover that a textbook you needed for next week - the one that costs €80 and has a three-week shipping delay - has just been made available as a free legal PDF by the publisher as part of some open-access initiative. You download it immediately.

Your phone buzzes. You fall asleep almost immediately, which never happens. You sleep for exactly the right number of hours. You do not wake up once. In the morning, the shower is the perfect temperature on the first try. Your tram arrives exactly as you reach the stop. Not a minute before, not a minute after. You didn't even check the schedule. You just walked up and there it was, like the universe had been holding it for you.

In the minutes before class starts, you're alone in the student lounge adjusting your hair, which was perfect when you woke up and has only gotten better. You give credit to the universe for the numerous strokes of luck that have occurred, but also take a moment to give credit to yourself for your quick wit and friendly conversational skill. You go to pat yourself on the back for doing a good job the previous night, but instead a different hand pats you on the back. You look and you see that it is in fact the luminous, divine hand of the ghost of Yuri Gagarin, who then daps you up, does a sick-ass kick flip on his skateboard, shreds an awesome riff on his electric guitar, and then ascends back into heaven in a shaft of light while giving you finger guns. Hell yeah. 

Triple click on the ISU logo on the Start Screen.

Here's My Card Ending.title: Here's My Card Ending
Here's My Card Ending.message: Success! You are now connected on LinkedIn. A solid digital foundation. It soon surpasses 500 connections, which you lord over your fellow classmates with glee, shaking your phone high above their heads and taunting them with your superior networking skills. You also made very nice conversation with Dr. Bennet. Congratulations!

Polite Conversationalist Ending.title: Polite Conversationalist Ending
Polite Conversationalist Ending.message:
Dr. Bennet gives you a smile and a nod. 'For sure. Enjoy your evening.'

As you walk back to the snack table for some recovery cheese, you decide you felt good about that interaction! It was a lovely conversation, for the most part, and you felt like you were charming and polite. You think she liked you.

But you're trying to figure out where in there you were supposed to ask for an internship, or share your LinkedIn; the time just never felt right. You suppose that asking for things like that is always going to be at the cost of a little awkwardness. Maybe next time you should just go for it.

Pity Party Ending.title: Pity Party Ending
Pity Party Ending.message: Dr. Bennet smiles, thanks you for your time, and then gets caught up in an adjacent conversation. You'd complain, but you literally told her to go talk to someone else. You put yourself down so hard and were so self-deprecating that that you convinced Dr. Bennet you are actually not a good student, which wasn't true from the beginning. Darius claps you on the back and gives you some food before telling you to keep your head up. He explains that if you're at ISU, you don't need to prove yourself to people you're networking with, since they already know you have to be pretty smart to be a student here. She just wants to know who you are and what you do. You'll get 'em next time; just be confident.

Back Away Slowly Ending.title: Back Away Slowly Ending
Back Away Slowly Ending.message: She nods with a forced smile, and then walks away from you without saying goodbye. I wonder what could have made her so uncomfortable.

Lost Momentum Ending.title: Lost Momentum Ending
Lost Momentum Ending.message: The conversation kind of just fizzles out and dies. Dr. Bennet gives you a smile and a nod, and then someone else comes by, and she starts to chat with them instead. Gotta learn to give her something to work with.

Aw Hell Naw Ending.title: Aw Hell Naw Ending
Aw Hell Naw Ending.message: Dr. Bennet seems to be mad! Congratulations, you've made her mad. That's not easy. She's pretty reasonable, so we're proud of the hard work you put in to get here.

Creeped Out Ending.title: Creeped Out Ending
Creeped Out Ending.message:
You made Dr. Bennet uncomfortable enough that she will not engage in conversation with you for the rest of the evening.

Can't imagine why.

Hammered Ending.title: Hammered Ending
Hammered Ending.message:
You're extremely drunk. Visibly. You're barely standing upright, and in a moment of imbalance you stumble and pitch sideways. You body-slam an ISU board member HARD, who body-slams the dean of students, and another, and another, and somehow a horrible domino effect of human bodies knocks down every single person in the room. It finally ends with your good friend Ken slamming headfirst into Felicette's statue. 

Everyone watches in horror as the statue wobbles, and then falls and breaks into several pieces on the tile below. 

Suddenly, the monstrous spectral ghost of Felicette the Cat springs from the wreckage. All the lights flicker out. In a deep, echoing French accent, Felicette swears to haunt ISU and its students forever, but says that you in particular are gonna get targeted. 

For the rest of your life, every single time you put a glass of wine down on a table, a ghostly paw knocks it over the side. Also you didn't get an internship.

Rage Quit Ending.title: Rage Quit Ending
Rage Quit Ending.message: You got so angry that you stormed out and simply went home. You feel vindicated, but you also feel unemployed.

Injury 1 Ending.title: Injury 1 Ending
Injury 1 Ending.message: You go to square up, but Dr. Bennet uses her hand on your wrist to push you away from her just a little bit. If you'd been sober you would have caught yourself - or maybe not be in this situation at all - but instead you trip on your own foot and fall backwards hard, hitting your head on the slate tiles beneath you. You wake up 4 minutes later, and Dr. Bennet is not there. Your concussion prevents you from doing any sort of networking for many weeks, but Dr. Bennet probably isn't going to be returning to ISU for a while anyway.

Injury 2 Ending.title: Injury 2 Ending
Injury 2 Ending.message:
You move your tongue close to your wrist to lick her hand to get her to let go. She lets go before you can get the chance - but you've been trying to pull your hands out of her grasp with so much force that as soon as she lets go, you accidentally punch yourself hard in the face. 

The force of your punch makes you stagger, and you fall backward forcefully on your butt. It doesn't hurt in the moment, or when Darius throws you over his shoulder and drags you out the doors, or even later that night, but it sure does hurt in the morning. 

Days later, the doctor says that he's never seen the specific combination of a broken tailbone AND a broken nose, and asks you how it happened. You're too embarrassed to say.

Injury 3 Ending.title: Injury 3 Ending
Injury 3 Ending.message:
You bend your knees and pull your hands away as HARD as you can to get Dr. Bennet to let go. Thankfully, she does - but you've been pulling back with so much force that when she frees your wrists, you accidentally punch yourself in the face.

It's not that having a double black eye prevents you from networking in the following weeks, but it definitely makes future employers wonder how you got hurt. If they haven't heard about the famous fight you had with Dr. Bennet, which most of them have.

Injury 4 Ending.title: Injury 4 Ending
Injury 4 Ending.message:
In your head, this goes perfectly, and you raise your leg to roundhouse kick her in the shoulder, swiftly disarming her, and then she's so impressed by your moves that she gives you the personal cell phone number of the director of NASA. In reality, you try to pick your leg up off the ground, but you miss and kick the table next to you instead, causing it to collapse and everything on it to fall and spill. When she pushes you away hard, your leg comes back down and you step on your other foot, and then YOU trip and fall hard on your shoulder, which dislocates with an intriguing pop.

Dr. Bennet quickly sets your shoulder joint back into place, but that's the last favor she ever does for you.

Dr. Wotring Ending.title: Dr. Wotring Ending
Dr. Wotring Ending.message:
In the middle of all of this, from behind Dr. Bennet you see your esteemed professor, Dr. Virginia Wotring.

She gives you a look that could fry an egg, and says, 'You know, I think you're done networking for the evening.'

Then she presses two fingers to her temple, and you spontaneously combust into a pile of pure carbon.

Run Away Home Ending.title: Run Away Home Ending
Run Away Home Ending.message:
You panic and run home without stopping. Which is crazy, because usually you take the tram, but you actually managed to RUN all the way back home. 

You get to your door and finally breathe normally before realizing you left your keys at school. Probably should have taken a second to think before sprinting away.

But Why Though Ending.title: But Why Though Ending
But Why Though Ending.message:
You send Elizabeth Bennet's husband further down the street. But, like, it's - like, he's gonna get to the end of the street and then see that ISU isn't there, and then probably pull out his phone and look on Google Maps and just... immediately figure it out. Or he'll just call his wife. I'm not sure what kind of punishment you thought you were giving out, but it's sort of just a minor inconvenience for Mr. Bennet, who honestly seemed pretty nice.

They're probably gonna talk later in bed about you being a strange douchebag. Good work!

Lyft Ending.title: Lyft Ending
Lyft Ending.message: He laughs out loud. A less understanding person might have simply left you outside and walked in to join his wife, but good, kind Brock Bennet takes pity on you and orders you a Lyft, since clearly you're shitfaced. Even though you threw a cube of cheese at his wife's head. He waits with you until it comes and tells you about the time he got too drunk at a formal event during his own Master's program. He seems like a cool guy; it's a shame you have zero recollection of meeting him.

Uber Ending.title: Uber Ending
Uber Ending.message: Instead of slapping the shit out of you, which would have been entirely justified, Brock Bennet takes pity on you and orders you an Uber, since clearly you're shitfaced. Even though you threw a cube of cheese at his wife's head and then called her a bitch. He seems like a cool guy. It's a shame you have zero recollection of meeting him.

Mr. Bennet Ending.title: Mr. Bennet Ending
Mr. Bennet Ending.message: Mr. Bennet turns out to be an exceedingly chill guy. He directs you to Darius, who makes you sit in the lounge and drink a full bottle of water, and then at the end of the night he comes by to make sure you're not about to throw up on yourself. Turns out that Dr. Bennet was baffled by your cheese throwing, but also thought it was the funniest thing in the world. Mr. Bennet also tells you about the time HE got hammered at a faculty event during his grad years, and you all laugh about it. You might not have made a perfect impression, but they'll at least remember you.

Go Home Ending.title: Go Home Ending
Go Home Ending.message: You go home. You're just done with the evening. Fair enough; it was a lot. Networking isn't easy. You'll get 'em next time.

Task Failed Successfully Ending.title: Task Failed Successfully Ending
Task Failed Successfully Ending.message:
She grins, and pulls out her phone. 'I know a guy. I hope he's not in the middle of something right now.'
The guy she knows turns out to be Dr. Jim Head, a lunar geology professor at a university, who is apparently in the middle of eating lunch at his desk when Dr. Bennet suddenly FaceTimes him. He appears on screen with the expression of a kindly older man who wasn't expecting a phone call.

'Elizabeth?' he says. 'Everything okay?'

'Quick question,' Dr. Bennet says immediately. 'Do volcanoes count as rock science?'

There is a long pause. Dr. Head slowly lowers the sandwich.

'…What?'

You lean into frame before she can poison the well. 'Okay, my point is that volcanoes are obviously more than just rocks. There's chemistry and fluid dynamics, too.'

'Volcanoes are geology, so I'd say they are... yeah, volcanoes are rock science.' Dr. Head says, still very confused.

Dr. Bennet folds her arms with visible satisfaction. 'Thank you, Dr. Head.'

You refuse to surrender, and your original goal of asking for an internship is lost. Dr. Bennet shall not win this argument. 'That's reductive, though. A volcano isn't just sitting there being a rock. It's DOING things.'

Dr. Head stares at you over the top of his glasses with the patient expression of a professor deciding whether this is a teachable moment or a cry for help.

'Well,' he says carefully, 'volcanology is interdisciplinary.'

You immediately point at the phone. 'Exactly.'

Dr. Bennet says, 'Wait, Jim, no. We're trying to prove ____ WRONG.'

'I mean,' he continues, now fully trapped in the conversation, 'you could say that there's geophysics, thermodynamics, chemistry...' He trails off.

You turn toward Dr. Bennet with a smirk you recognize is slightly inappropriate, given that she is such a higher authority figure than you. But your vindication comes from a DIFFERENT higher authority figure. 'He's siding with me, I think.'

'He did not SIDE with you.'

'I heard what I heard.'

Dr. Head takes another bite of his sandwich. 'To be clear, volcanoes are fundamentally geological structures.'

'But not only geological,' you fire back instantly. 

'...Technically,' he admits, 'the chemistry thing is not entirely wrong...'

You throw both hands into the air 'Dr. Head,' you ask, 'would you say I was technically correct?'

He chews thoughtfully for a moment. '…Against my better judgment, yes... are you a volcanologist yourself?'

You confirm this to him, and he thinks for another moment before going, 'I'm sorry, what did you say your name was?'

Despite her feigned exasperation, in a show of good sportsmanship, Dr. Bennet allows you to keep her phone for as long as it takes you to ask Dr. Head for his contact information. Dr. Head has many more thoughts about your argument, and you both agree to continue the debate later on a proper Zoom call, and also perhaps discuss the possibility of him hosting your thesis. You're not... really sure how you got here, but you're not complaining whatsoever.

Too Nonchalant Ending.title: Too Nonchalant Ending
Too Nonchalant Ending.message:
Just that moment, she gets a phone call. It's from her husband, he's been stuck in traffic and is still trying to get there. You let her take the call.

When she gets off the phone, she is immediately swept up into a different conversation. You wait around for her the whole evening, but you fear that because you weren't that excited about her offer, that she totally forgot that she was going to give it to you. But it doesn't matter. You'll catch her before the night is over. 

You wait, and you wait, and you drink wine, which is a diuretic, and you wait some more, and then you have to pee, so you go to the bathroom. When you come back, she's gone; you learn that her husband's rental car got a flat tire, and she had to take someone's car to go rescue him. Lots of troubles on the road for Mr. Bennet. You ask if they're coming back, but it's already so late that everyone says she's probably not going to. Damn. You were SO CLOSE.

Sorry, champ. I think you came off a little TOO uninterested.

Alpha Male Ending.title: Lone Wolf
Alpha Male Ending.message:
You get down on all fours and start howling, to establish dominance. You feel so strong. By turning down Dr. Bennet's offer, you have officially become the most commanding person in the room. You prowl around on the floor of Pioneer Hall, snarling and biting at anyone who dares get too close. They look at you in fear and confusion, probably because they're so intimidated by your alpha status. They can smell the power emanating off of you. 

Darius walks over to you. 'What the fuck are you doing?'

He won't understand. None of them will understand. But you don't need them. You don't need anybody. You're a lone wolf. 

You go outside, pee on the side of the building with one leg all the way up in the air, and then you go home.

Student Athlete Ending.title: Student Athlete Ending
Student Athlete Ending.message:
You run way back, even when literally everyone protests. 'Don't worry!' You say. 'Don't worry. I've been practicing with the lounge sports equipment.'

Everyone SCREAMS at you to not do whatever it is you're about to do, but things that have been set in motion cannot be undone. You get WAY far away, on the other side of the room, and nobody can run up to you fast enough to stop the incredible spiral throw you make with Dr. Bennet's phone. You yell, 'Catch it!'

It's a good throw. A great throw. But you overshoot by a mile, and it THUDS against the opposite wall, and then falls to the ground with another, louder CRACK. Silence follows for a harrowing moment and a half.

Everyone slowly gathers around it, and you push through the crowd to see that the screen has become completely separated from the body of the device. Dr. Bennet is holding it in her hands like it's a dying songbird, shaking her head with disbelief, and saying, 'I didn't ever back up those photos...'

She turns to look at you with a fiery rage she doesn't bother to hide. Neither her nor anybody else speaks to you for the rest of the evening, except Darius, who informs you that you may be clinically stupid and should be checked by a doctor.

Oh, and also you forgot to add yourself on LinkedIn. Not sure how you bungled this so bad in the final hour. Wisdom is fast, but your baseball pitch is faster.

Own Goal Ending.title: Own Goal
Own Goal Ending.message: Self-deprecation is like nutmeg; you really only want a sprinkle of it to be appealing. Otherwise you risk overwhelming the meal, you know? Or just seeming like you don't like yourself, which makes people wonder if maybe they shouldn't like you either. Even if you genuinely think your background is uninteresting, you HAVE to pretend like it's interesting. At a certain point, if you keep beating yourself up to her, Dr. Bennet has no choice but to take you at your word, and you literally just told her your background is not worth listening to. If she asks for it, just tell her about it, and she can judge for herself. Now she's moved on to speaking to another person. Try advocating for yourself a little more next time. Dr. Bennet doesn't have to give you anything, but you always have the right to ask her if she has any opportunities. And you do deserve them, even if you think you don't. Keep your head up, champion!

Mysterious Man Ending.title: Mysterious Man Ending
Mysterious Man Ending.message:
Dr. Bennet walks away at a speed you are too drunk to interpret as a 50/50 blend of frightened and pissed off. Plus, your voice has increased in volume without you noticing, and you also hadn't noticed everyone else in the room stop their conversation to look at the source of the noise. Most of them look weirded out, and you can see Darius quickly approaching you from the corner. But a different stranger in the back also starts clapping emphatically, smiling and wiping tears from his face. You look - he's got a long white beard, a whimsical hat, and a cloak the color of a smoked, spicy chorizo sausage. Despite this odd attire, nobody else seems to notice him. All nerves about social interaction now lost, you feel somehow that you MUST speak to him. 

You approach, and he grasps your hand with both of his and shakes it so hard your whole body moves. 'My pupil!' he cries with joy. 'My salami apprentice! You have made me proud on this evening. Oh, you have done what you said you could not do, and that is speak to new people for the first time. I am overjoyed to hear you spread the good word of salami. Your words are like mustard on dark rye, my student,' the old man says, gripping your shoulders with terrifying strength. 'Joyful. Proud. Slightly upsetting to weak people.'
Tiny slices of cured meat hang from his belt like arcane charms.
'I'm sorry,' you say. 'Who... are you?'

The old man gasps theatrically and places one hand over his heart.

'WHO am I? WHO am I?' He leans in close and says, 'Why, I am Garibaldo the Preserved! The Umami King of Alsace-Lorraine. Keeper of the Nine Salts. Duke of Soppressata. Excommunicated from four vegetarian co-ops. I am the Salami Wizard.'

He bows deeply. 

You scoff. '...You're not real. I'm drunk and this is a hallucination.'

His eyebrows wiggle mischeviously. 'ALL magic sounds fake before the nitrates awaken. I have watched over this school since 1999. I was in the SSP.'

Before you can respond, he reaches into his cloak and produces an unrealistically long stick of salami, at least a meter and a half in length. It hums faintly. 

'The Staff of Genoa,' he whispers reverently, eyes wide and crazy. 'Do not point it at the moon.'

Suddenly, thunder rumbles in the distance, despite the clear skies outside. He sighs, and kneels to put his hand on your shoulder.

'My apprentice,' he says solemnly, 'My time here is short, and my message is urgent and personal. The world grows dark. People fear sodium. They buy low-fat turkey sticks, and charcuterie boards are becoming over 50% hummus. We stand at the edge of an age of blandness. You and I have the power to change this. To shape the salami path forward as we desire. But to get the things you want, the future you seek - you must have COURAGE. You must ASK for the good things that deep in your salami soul, you know you deserve.'

He slams the salami staff against the floor. A great portal appears behind him, and he says, 'I have searched many years for one brave enough to speak publicly and drunkenly of cured meats without shame. Tonight... I found you. There is hope yet. You have found your courage. Use it well next time.'

You swallow. 'I dunno, man. I'm just kinda hammered.'

He hands you the staff, and it buzzes and crackles as it enters your grasp. It feels... right. Like you had its power inside you all along.

The wizard turns to leave. 'My scholar of salami, you need no elixirs to speak with wit and charm.' He winks, and then the portal closes and he is gone, and you look down and you're holding a mop handle, and a new, beautiful sense of courage has blossomed in your chest. 

Only then does Darius grab your shoulders and bring you outside, and you struggle against him loudly and continue yammering about your unquenchable desire to follow the wizard to the salami dimension. Perhaps tonight was not your night, and who knows what was in your wine, but somehow you have gained a power that makes you think that if you tried again, you would do it all perfectly.

Rapid Unscheduled Decompression Ending.title: Rapid Unscheduled Decompression Ending
Rapid Unscheduled Decompression Ending.message:
Uh oh. You fucked up. You fucked up big time. There's a split second of hope where you think you can conceal it, but there's not really any concealing it. It sounds like someone loudly revving a motorcycle, if the motorcycle was also uncomfortably wet and smelled like poop.

It's pretty apparent to everyone, but most of all to Dr. Elizabeth Bennet, that you have just shit your pants. Bad. And seemingly on purpose.

It echoes across the hall, and all conversation stops dead. Not gradually, either. One second the room is alive with chatter, and the next it is as silent as the night is black. Somewhere across Pioneer Hall, a spoon softly clinks against a coffee cup like the final note of a funeral dirge.

A look of profound scientific curiosity crosses Dr. Bennet's face.

You shift slightly in place and instantly regret it. The smell rolls outward through the crowd. Someone mutters, 'Dear God.'

Dr. Bennet blinks twice. 'Are you... okay?'

You attempt to answer, but your body has abandoned networking formalities. A sound escapes you that can only be described as a dying accordion. Two other students near the snack table physically recoil.

Darius appears seemingly out of nowhere, guided by the truest friendship anyone could ever possibly offer. 'Hey, bud,' he says carefully, maintaining the body language of someone defusing a bomb, 'I think maybe we should get you somewhere private.'

You try to take a step toward the exit. This proves catastrophic, but it must be done.

You are escorted from the event at a speed normally reserved for medical emergencies. Behind you, conversations slowly resume. Dr. Bennet stands there for a while longer, not interacting with anybody, staring at the floor with a look of utter confusion. Processing the event would take everyone a few days, at least, and your pants, flung into the marsh behind the bike shed, are never to be seen again, dirtied beyond washing. It's hard to say if you ever truly recover.

Brock Blocked Ending.title: Brock Blocked
Brock Blocked Ending.message:
In this moment, God frowns upon you, and a man walks up to Dr. Bennet and puts his hand on her shoulder. 'Hey, Liz! Sorry I was late; traffic was an absolute nightmare. Who's this?'

Dr. Bennet introduces you, and you learn that the man is Brock Bennet, the husband of the woman you just tried to seduce. If he knows what you were doing, he's playing it really cool, which is somehow even worse than him yelling at you.

His hand doesn't leave her shoulder for the rest of the evening. Dr. Bennet is distantly polite, and the rest of your conversation goes fine, but she doesn't give you anybody's contact information. And she definitely wasn't going to give you hers.

Injury 7 Ending.title: Injury 7 Ending
Injury 7 Ending.message:
It gets stuck. Your fist gets stuck in your mouth. You make a huge scene and panic, and Dr. Bennet tries to stabilize your arm. 'You need to calm down so your jaw muscles relax.'

You panic even more, and nothing she does or says to get you to stop pulling is of any use. 

Finally, you pull so hard that when you finally unwedge your fist from your mandible, you fling your head backwards, and you crash horizontally into a folding table which collapses under your weight. You are covered in cheese, hummus, wine, and diet soda.

Despite Dr. Bennet's concern for the wellbeing of your hand and your jaw, she seems to express significantly less concern for the wellbeing of your career, because she does not give you an internship. You can't really blame her; you don't know for sure, but you're pretty certain that these are not the kinds of things that happen to people who become astronauts.

Tablecloth Ending.title: Tablecloth Ending
Tablecloth Ending.message: In your head, this goes really well. The tablecloth theoretically should be pulled away cleanly, and all of the glasses and plates and other items should stay right in place on the table, due to inertia. In reality, as soon as you yank the cloth, you fuck it up so bad that all of the plates and cups are violently ejected from the table, and spilled onto other surfaces like the floor, your outfit, and Dr. Elizabeth Bennet. You aren't really sure what emotions she's experiencing, but you're certain she's not impressed. You apologize profusely and then go to wash off in the lounge sink. When you come back out, Dr. Bennet has left for the evening. You shake your fist at the sky and curse Isaac Newton for leading you astray about inertia, and wonder how your flawless plan could have gone so awry. Darius doesn't let you hear the end of it for the rest of the year. Neither does anybody else.

EarlyExit.title: You Didn't Even Start The Game
EarlyExit.message:
You looked at Ella's networking simulator, felt a sudden, sharp wave of existential dread, and decided that the only winning move was not to play. Hey, nice War Games reference. 

You turn off your computer and go for a walk. The air is fresh, the birds are singing, and you are at peace. Jobs are for nerds, anyway.

DariusTime.title: Darius Time
DariusTime.message: You walk back up to Darius, hoping he can do the hard work and butter up Dr. Bennet for you, but before you can say anything, Darius steals your phone. He runs away giggling. You give chase, but he's too fast, and he takes so many pictures that your phone maxes out its storage and then runs out of battery and dies. What were you expecting?
